name: Georgia Residential and General Contractors License Search

agency.name: Georgia Secretary of State Licensing Division

updateFrequency: unknown; verify against the official licensing search before relying on freshness assumptions
licenseTypesIncluded: Georgia Residential Basic and Residential Light Commercial contractor licenses
Georgia Commercial General Contractor and Commercial General Contractor Limited Tier licenses exposed by the official search
knownExclusions: Traditional specialty work such as roofing, painting, drywall, and other listed scopes is exempt from this residential/general contractor license when performed within the specialty.
Electrical, plumbing, conditioned-air, low-voltage, and utility credentials are governed by the separate State Construction Industry Licensing Board.
Local permits, business registrations, asbestos credentials, and other state construction-industry boards are not represented by this source entry.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Prefer official bulk options if discovered before considering lookup automation.
publicRecordsNotes: Official lookup source metadata only. Absence from this source is not proof that a license does not exist elsewhere.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: No official bulk download has been confirmed in this phase.
researchNotes: The board requires licenses for compensated residential and commercial general contractor services while recognizing statutory exemptions for traditional specialty work. The shared GOALS lookup remains a manual handoff until a stable machine interface is reviewed.
verificationCaveats: A specialty contractor may lawfully operate within a recognized specialty without this residential/general contractor license, while separately regulated trades still require their own credentials.
No matching record means no match in the selected official source at the checked time, not proof that no license or lawful specialty authorization exists.
verificationNotes: No supported sync or verification adapter is exposed while the shared interactive portal lacks a reviewed stable machine interface.
maintainerNotes: Keep Residential Basic, Residential Light Commercial, Commercial General Contractor, and Limited Tier distinctions visible. Do not imply all Georgia specialty work requires this board license.

researchEvidence.note: Official public source, access shape, and current automation constraints reviewed.
Official FAQ states compensated residential and commercial general contractor services require a license and identifies the four license categories.
Official policy identifies traditional specialty work exempt from the residential/general contractor license while preserving separately regulated trade requirements.

blocker.summary: No stable, documented machine-readable official interface is currently suitable for an offline-tested adapter.
